req.title: Beneficiary management
req.statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-011 mandates that adding a beneficiary produces a pending state with a 24-hour cooling-off period. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive – a valid, unique beneficiary is accepted with 201 and status=pending; (2) NEG negative – a duplicate account number triggers a 409 conflict; (3) BVA boundary – the beneficiary status exactly at the 24-hour mark transitions from pending to active, verifying the boundary of the cooling-off window; (4) STC negative – an attempt to use a pending beneficiary as a transfer destination before cooling-off expires is rejected, confirming the state machine blocks premature use. The violation fix: test[3] had kind=negative and heuristic=STC, which is valid per schema rules since STC can be used with negative kind to represent a state-transition that must NOT occur; however the rule-checker flagged STC as only valid for non-negative kinds. To comply, the heuristic for test[3] is changed to NEG while retaining the state-transition rationale in the trace, preserving full coverage of the cooling-off enforcement behaviour.

tests.name: test_add_valid_beneficiary_enters_pending_state
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
Beneficiary account number does not already exist for this customer
actions: POST /beneficiaries with a valid, unique account payload
Inspect HTTP response status and JSON body
expected: Response body contains a non-null beneficiary_id
Response body contains cooling_off_expires_at timestamp ~24 h in the future
trace: R-FIN-011: beneficiary saved in pending state
R-FIN-011: 24-hour cooling-off period begins on add
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_add_valid_beneficiary_enters_pending_state(auth_client, unique_account_number):
    payload = {
        "account_number": unique_account_number,
        "account_name": "Alice Example",
        "bank_code": "TESTBANK"
    }
    resp = auth_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending", f"Expected pending, got {body.get('status')}"
    assert body.get("beneficiary_id") is not None
    expires_at = datetime.fromisoformat(body["cooling_off_expires_at"])
    now_utc = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    expected_expiry = now_utc + timedelta(hours=24)
    delta = abs((expires_at - expected_expiry).total_seconds())
    assert delta < 60, f"cooling_off_expires_at drift too large: {delta}s"

tests.name: test_add_duplicate_beneficiary_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A beneficiary with the same account number already exists for this customer (seeded via fixture)
actions: POST /beneficiaries with an account_number that is already registered for the customer
Inspect HTTP response status and JSON body
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating duplicate account
trace: R-FIN-011: error pathway – 409 if duplicate account
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_add_duplicate_beneficiary_returns_409(auth_client, existing_beneficiary_account):
    payload = {
        "account_number": existing_beneficiary_account,
        "account_name": "Duplicate User",
        "bank_code": "TESTBANK"
    }
    resp = auth_client.post("/beneficiaries", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body, "Response must contain error detail"
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("code", ""))).lower()
    assert "duplicate" in error_text or "already" in error_text or "conflict" in error_text, \
        f"Unexpected error message: {error_text}"

tests.name: test_beneficiary_status_at_exactly_24h_boundary
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A beneficiary was created exactly 24 hours ago (clock-controlled via fixture or test-clock API)
System test-clock is available to advance time
actions: Advance system test-clock to T+24h using <advance_clock_fixture>
expected: Beneficiary is no longer in pending state at the 24-hour boundary
trace: R-FIN-011: 24-hour cooling-off period – boundary at exactly 24 h
BVA: at T=24h the state transitions from pending to active
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_beneficiary_status_at_exactly_24h_boundary(
    auth_client, beneficiary_created_24h_ago, advance_clock
):
    beneficiary_id = beneficiary_created_24h_ago["beneficiary_id"]
    cooling_off_expires_at = beneficiary_created_24h_ago["cooling_off_expires_at"]
    advance_clock(to_iso=cooling_off_expires_at)
    resp = auth_client.get(f"/beneficiaries/{beneficiary_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active", \
        f"Expected active at 24h boundary, got {body.get('status')}"

tests.name: test_pending_beneficiary_cannot_receive_transfer
preconditions: Customer is authenticated and holds a valid JWT
A beneficiary exists with status=pending (cooling-off not yet expired)
Customer account has sufficient funds for a transfer
actions: POST /transfers targeting the pending beneficiary_id before the 24-hour cooling-off expires
Inspect HTTP response status and JSON body
expected: Response body contains an error indicating the beneficiary is in a cooling-off period
trace: R-FIN-011: 24-hour cooling-off period before beneficiary can receive transfers
NEG/STC: state=pending must block the transfer trigger; transfer is only valid from state=active
tests.executable:
import pytest

ALLOWED_BLOCK_STATUSES = {403, 422, 400}

def test_pending_beneficiary_cannot_receive_transfer(
    auth_client, pending_beneficiary
):
    beneficiary_id = pending_beneficiary["beneficiary_id"]
    transfer_payload = {
        "beneficiary_id": beneficiary_id,
        "amount": "10.00",
        "currency": "USD",
        "reference": "cooling-off-transfer-test"
    }
    resp = auth_client.post("/transfers", json=transfer_payload)
    assert resp.status_code in ALLOWED_BLOCK_STATUSES, \
        f"Expected transfer to be blocked, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body.get("error", body.get("message", ""))).lower()
    assert any(kw in error_text for kw in ["cooling", "pending", "not active", "restricted"]), \
        f"Expected cooling-off error, got: {error_text}"
